cross_monitor_signals.esa: US National Policy Framework advances tech sovereignty

weekly_brief_narrative: The White House National Policy Framework for AI, released March 20, 2026, dominates as the week's lead signal due to its push for unified federal legislation, bridging volatile executive actions with durable law amid enterprise compliance pressures. Frontier capabilities (I1) show tool-level advances in agentic monitoring like Datatron's sabotage suites, but no new model releases from labs. Regulatory actions (I2) center on US framework proposals and EU AI Act tool readiness, with governance platforms automating compliance for 2026 deadlines. Safety incidents (I3) remain prospective, focusing on shadow AI and drift risks without reported failures. Geopolitics (I4) inferred from US standardization trends; investments (I5) evident in enterprise governance scaling; cross-monitor (I6) quiet on FIMI or conflicts. EU AI Act on track for August prohibited systems enforcement, supported by tools for Article 12 logs; US mixes EO durability with legislative momentum; UK and China stable without updates. Watch EU AI Office delegated acts in Q2, US congressional response to Framework by mid-May, and any lab benchmarks post-April lull; monitor Tier 1 blogs for I1 jumps and Tier 2 for I3 incidents.
